articles.description:
McDonald's has released its new, limited-time Buffalo Ranch and new, limited-time Chips Ahoy! Frappe. While the official launch isn't until October 28, 2025, some locations are already serving the new sauce and drink.

McDonald's Buffalo Ranch is available as a dipping sauce and is featured in the Bacon Buffalo Ranch McCrispy sandwich and Buffalo Ranch Snack Wrap.
The Bacon Buffalo Ranch McCrispy is available in a regular and Deluxe version. Here's what's in each version of the sandwich:
- Bacon Buffalo Ranch McCrispy - A McCrispy chicken filet, Buffalo Ranch, applewood-smoked bacon, and crinkle-cut pickles, served on a toasted potato roll. It costs $6.59 near me in Los Angeles.
- Bacon Buffalo Ranch Deluxe McCrispy - A McCrispy chicken filet, Buffalo Ranch sauce, applewood-smoked bacon, lettuce, and tomato, served on a toasted potato roll. The sandwich is priced at $7.19 near me.
The Buffalo Ranch Snack Wrap features a McCrispy Strip, Buffalo Ranch sauce, shredded cheese, and shredded lettuce, wrapped in a soft flour tortilla (i.e., it's the Ranch Snack Wrap, but with Buffalo Ranch sauce instead of Ranch). It's priced at $2.99.
If the trio seems familiar, it's the same menu as for the outgoing Special Edition Gold Sauce, only with Buffalo Ranch.
You can also get a dipping cup of the new sauce with an order of McCrispy Strips or Chicken McNuggets. You can also purchase the sauce cup separately (prices vary, but it costs $0.29 near me).
The new Chips Ahoy! Frappe is a coffee frappe blended with chocolate chip cookie syrup and Chips Ahoy! cookie pieces, topped with whipped cream and more Chips Ahoy! cookie pieces. Prices vary, but a small Chips Ahoy! Frappe costs $3.39 here in Los Angeles.

Taco Bell is testing new Gordita Crunch Sliders for a limited time at participating locations in the Nashville area.

The new menu item features a set of two snack-sized pillowy flatbreads folded around crispy white-corn shells with a layer of melted three-cheese blend to hold them together. Nestled inside the shells is a filling of your choice of seasoned beef, slow-roasted chicken, or grilled, marinated steak, plus Creamy Chipotle sauce and pico de gallo. They range from $4.49 to $5.49 (varying with location) for the chicken or steak versions and $3.49 for the seasoned beef version.
While essentially a mini rendition of the Cheesy Gordita Crunch, the Gordita Crunch Sliders sport a slightly different taco shell (made of white corn instead of yellow corn) and a different combination of fillings (the Cheesy Gordita Crunch is filled with seasoned beef, shredded lettuce, shredded cheddar cheese, and Spicy Ranch).
Gordita Crunch Sliders are also available as part of a $9 Discovery Luxe Cravings Box and a $6.99 Snack Trio, which is an in-app-only item available at select locations.
The $9 Discovery Luxe Cravings Box includes a set of two Gordita Crunch Sliders, a Nacho Cheese Doritos Locos Taco, a Crunchy Taco, Cinnamon Twists, and a medium fountain drink.
The $6.99 Snack Trio includes a set of two Gordita Crunch Sliders, Nacho Fries with nacho cheese sauce, and two Cinnabon Delights.

The seasonal Gobbler is back at Wawa convenience stores for the 2025 fall season.

The Thanksgiving-friendly sandwich features hot turkey in gravy (you can opt for cold sliced turkey [not in gravy]) that can be customized with several fixings, including cranberry sauce, stuffing, and mashed potatoes. New options this year include Double Stuffed Gobblers and Gobbler Wraps.
Double Stuffed Gobblers are hot turkey sandwiches with either a double portion of stuffing or a combination of stuffing and either mashed potatoes (pictured) or mashed sweet potatoes. Like the regular Gobbler, you can select your roll, cheese, and spreads (cranberry is recommended).

Dunkin's 2025 Halloween menu has arrived. The seasonal menu includes the launch of the new Candy Bar Signature Latte along with the return of the Spider Donut and the Halloween Munchkins Bucket.

The new Candy Bar Signature Latte features espresso and your choice of milk mixed with a blend of chocolate, toffee, pretzel, and peanut butter flavors, topped with whipped cream, a mocha drizzle, and crumbled candy bar pieces. You can get the new beverage hot or iced.
The Spider Donut is a donut with purple icing, topped with a Glazed Chocolate Munchkins donut hole and decorated with chocolate drizzle "spider legs" and vanilla-flavored icing "eyes."
This year's Halloween Munchkins Bucket gets a new look with a purple Spider Donut-inspired lid on a black bucket featuring a Halloween motif that includes Spider Donut illustrations as well as depictions of Munchkins dressed in costume. The bucket comes filled with 50 Munchkins, including Halloween-themed Munchkins covered in chocolate-and-orange sprinkles (Dunkin's classic Chocolate, Vanilla, and Strawberry Frosted donuts also get the chocolate-and-orange sprinkles instead of their regular sprinkles for the Halloween season).
Dunkin's 2025 Halloween menu is available now for a limited time at participating locations nationwide.
Photo via Dunkin'.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Dunkin's 2025 Halloween Menu Arrives

articles.description:
Taco Bell's Flamin' Hot Grilled Cheese Burrito features Flamin' Hot Fritos, new Flamin' Hot Rice, seasoned beef, a three-cheese blend, Creamy Chipotle sauce, warm nacho cheese sauce, and reduced-fat sour cream inside a warm flour tortilla topped with grilled cheese.

The burrito costs $6.49 at my local Taco Bell, but is also available as part of a $9 Discovery Luxe Cravings Box that includes the burrito, a Chicken Chalupa Supreme, a Crunchy Taco, Chips & Nacho Cheese Sauce, and a medium fountain drink, so I paid the extra $2.50 for a lot more food.
According to Taco Bell, the Flamin' Hot Grilled Cheese Burrito is meant to be "cheesier" and "spicier" than its predecessor, the Beefy Crunch Burrito. With the extra layer of melted cheese on the outside, it's definitely cheesier but I can't say it's any spicier. While the Flamin' Hot Rice gave the fillings a bold red hue, I picked out some grains and found the flavor to be surprisingly mild given the visuals. Mostly, it just added texture and filler.
I took my paritcular burrito on a short drive home to eat it and the Fritos were throughly softened. They provided a bulk of the savory and spicy Flamin' Hot flavor as well as a hit of fried corn.



The sauces (Creamy Chipotle, nacho cheese, and sour cream) were mostly lost amid the Flamin' Hot, seasoned beef, and grilled cheese flavors.
Overall, I liked Taco Bell's Flamin' Hot Grilled Cheese Burrito a fair bit. The Flamin' Hot flavor goes well with the generally beefy cheesiness and there was a decent variety of textures. However, if you consider that they tested a $2 version of almost the same burrito earlier this year, minus the grilled cheese (the price is in line with the regular Grilled Cheese Burrito, however), the Flamin' Hot Grilled Cheese Burrito is quite pricey and makes getting the $9 combo a no-brainer.
Nutritional Info - Taco Bell Flamin' Hot Grilled Cheese Burrito
Calories - 730
Fat - 41g (Saturated Fat - 17g)
Sodium - 1270mg
Carbs - 65g (Sugar - 4g)
Protein - 26g
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Review: Taco Bell - Flamin' Hot Grilled Cheese Burrito

Taco Bell is considering a potential expansion of its Cantina Chicken Menu with its new Cantina Fajita test menu at participating locations in the Dallas, TX area.

The key feature of the menu is seasoned bell peppers and onions paired with either carne asada (steak) or slow-roasted chicken. As with the Cantina Menu, all items come with a packet of Avocado Verde Salsa. The Cantina Fajita menu includes:
- Cantina Fajita Bowl - A bed of seasoned rice topped with simmered black beans, pico de gallo, guacamole, reduced-fat sour cream, seasoned bell peppers and onions, a three-cheese blend, Creamy Jalapeno sauce, and a double portion of carne asada or slow-roasted chicken. Two small warm flour tortillas are included on the side. It's priced at $7.99 with either protein option.
- Cantina Fajita Grilled Cheese Burrito - Seasoned rice, carne asada or slow-roasted chicken, seasoned bell peppers and onions, Creamy Jalapeno sauce, and a three-cheese blend, wrapped in a warm flour tortilla and topped with a layer of grilled cheese. The suggested price for the burrito is $5.99.
- Cantina Fajita Grilled Soft Taco - A soft flour tortilla folded and grilled with carne asada or slow-roasted chicken, seasoned bell peppers and onions, Creamy Jalapeno sauce, and a three-cheese blend. It's priced at $3.49.
- Cantina Fajita Quesadilla - A large flour tortilla folded with carne asada or slow-roasted chicken, seasoned bell peppers and onions, Creamy Jalapeno sauce, and a three-cheese blend, and then grilled. It costs $6.49.
- Cantina Fajita Discovery Luxe Box - For $9, the box includes a Cantina Fajita Grilled Soft Taco (carne asada or slow-roasted chicken), Doritos Locos Taco, Beefy 5-Layer Burrito, Cinnamon Twists, and a medium fountain drink.
